content: We can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a linguistic 'reflection' (the double meaning of reflexion from the vocabulary entry) that borrows the autonomous dynamic memory design of the original Reflexion agent framework to fix the common flaw of single-perspective self-reflection: by grounding LLM self-reflection in the incrementally updated episodic memory of past failed outputs that Reflexion formalizes, self-contrast methods can draw more consistent inconsistent perspectives to improve reasoning.
